title: install --update does not propagate catalog config-registry additions (relation_types/invariants/block_kinds) to an existing substrate
description: installContext writes config via a verbatim spread ({...config, installed_from}), so new catalog config-registry entries (relation_types, invariants, block_kinds, lenses) are never merged into an existing substrate's config on install --update; adoptConception writes registries only on a skeleton. A catalog model addition (e.g. new governance relation_types/invariants) therefore reaches only fresh substrates; existing substrates never receive it. Distinct from FGAP-046 (schema clobber): this is non-propagation of config-registry additions, not destruction of edits.

evidence.reference: installContext writes config via writeConfig(cwd, {...config, installed_from}) — verbatim spread; no catalog-registry merge
adoptConception writes registries only on a skeleton (isSkeletonConfig guard)

impact: Any catalog vocabulary/invariant addition is delivered only to fresh installs; existing users must hand-amend-config to receive it — capping the value of every catalog release for the installed base.
proposed_resolution: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
